title: Renode CI jobs reference non-existent board 'nrf52840_lichen' — 2 jobs red
description:
Two CI jobs fail because they 'west build -b nrf52840_lichen', but nrf52840_lichen is NOT a real Zephyr board — lichen/boards/renode/nrf52840_lichen/ contains only Renode platform files (support/nrf52840_lichen.repl, .resc), the multi-node harness (test_mesh.py, run_multi_node.py, conftest.py), and a README. No board.yml/board.cmake/dts/Kconfig.board exists, so board resolution fails with 'Invalid BOARD' (boards.cmake:244). Confirmed via git: the commit that added it (3adc8cc2 'feat(renode): add board definitions') only added README + Renode .repl/.resc + Python harness + C# peripherals — never a Zephyr board definition.

Affected jobs:
1. .github/workflows/renode.yml 'Renode nRF52840 Boot + TX' — was failing on SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='0' (fixed in PR #2 to use the commit ts); now reaches board resolution. Changed -b to t_echo/nrf52840 (PR #2) which resolves the board, but then the puck app's Kconfig for the Renode console overlay/conf mismatches (CONFIG_LICHEN_NATIVE assigned y got n, ZCBOR/MCUMGR warnings-as-errors) — the renode_console.conf/overlay path is stale post-HWMv2.
2. .github/workflows/renode-firmware.yml 'Build Zephyr Firmware' — builds lora_ping and puck for -b nrf52840_lichen (invalid board), plus later steps reference build/lora_ping ELF.

Pre-existing infrastructure breakage from the HWMv2 migration, unrelated to the agent-fleet code or the T1000-E work. Fix requires reworking the Renode CI to build the ELF for a REAL nRF52840 board (e.g. the actual t_echo/nrf52840 or a dedicated nrf52840dk/nrf52840) that the Renode .repl/.resc platform models, then load that ELF in Renode. NOT VALIDATABLE in the current sandbox (no Renode installed). The T-Echo Renode console path also needs its overlay/conf refreshed for the current puck Kconfig.
close_reason: Board/build root cause fixed & confirmed green in b65e5078. renode-firmware.yml 'Build Zephyr Firmware' + single/multi-node jobs: SUCCESS (was building phantom -b nrf52840_lichen; now t_echo/nrf52840 + renode_console overlay/conf + new lora_ping t_echo zephyr,lora overlay + SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH). renode.yml 'Renode Platform Validation': success. renode.yml 'Renode nRF52840 Boot + TX' now builds+boots (test_mesh_boots PASSES) after fixing the west-update-on-cache-hit module drop. The remaining test_mesh_tx failure (0 LoRa transmissions into lichen-sim) is a DISTINCT emulation-layer issue in the SX1262 Renode model/bridge, refiled as yot8 — not a board problem. Neither job references the non-existent board anymore.
